# Clang 21 crash (Stack dump) when using _Atomic(struct) field access incorrectly in C code

**Author:** mariete1223
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/137867
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** clang:frontend, confirmed, crash, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-06-13T10:45:41Z

## Body

## Summary:

Clang 21 crashes with a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) when compiling C code that uses _Atomic(struct) and tries to load and dereference a member incorrectly.
Instead of reporting a clean error about invalid atomic access, the compiler crashes during semantic analysis (Sema::RefersToMemberWithReducedAlignment).


## Program

```
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pthread.h>
struct T {
 char value;
};
void* work(void* p) {
 struct T* t = (struct T*)p;
}
int main()
{
 _Atomic(struct T) t;
 while (!atomic_load(&t.value)->value)
  ;
 return t.value->value;
}
```

## To quickly reproduce

https://gcc.godbolt.org/z/d6GbsWjq5

Clang 21 crashes with a segmentation fault (SIGSEGV) when compiling C code that uses _Atomic(struct) and tries to load and dereference a member incorrectly.
Instead of reporting a clean error about invalid atomic access, the compiler crashes during semantic analysis (Sema::RefersToMemberWithReducedAlignment).

## Program

```
#include &lt;stdlib.h&gt;
#include &lt;pthread.h&gt;
struct T {
 char value;
};
void* work(void* p) {
 struct T* t = (struct T*)p;
}
int main()
{
 _Atomic(struct T) t;
 while (!atomic_load(&amp;t.value)-&gt;value)
  ;
 return t.value-&gt;value;
}
```

## To quickly reproduce

https://gcc.godbolt.org/z/d6GbsWjq5
</details>


---

### Comment 2 - shafik

Requires `-std=c2x` and optimization level is not required. Goes back to clang-15.

---

### Comment 3 - shafik

Is this fuzzer generated?

---

### Comment 4 - mariete1223

Yes, it is

---

### Comment 5 - mariete1223

I have uploaded recently a bunch of fuzzer generated crashes

---

### Comment 6 - shafik

Things to keep in mind. We receive a larger volume of bugs and we don't have the capacity to address all of them. So a large volume of fuzzer generated bugs won't likely be helpful, although a small number of high quality bugs can be useful. High quality is hard to define, but bugs that find regressions in a recent release will be some of the highest quality bugs.

Assertion builds are more useful in general b/c they normally crash earlier and the assertion usually is helpful in understanding the bug. Including the assertion is important.

Please also make an effort to search for duplicates or related bugs and mention them in the bug report. If it is an exact duplicate (the assertion and backtrace match perfectly) then just add your reproducer to the existing bug rather than creating a new bug report.

---

### Comment 7 - mariete1223

Thank you for the guidelines.
I tried to simplify the bugs found and check for duplicates, but I realize I haven't fully followed all the steps you mentioned. I will take this as a checklist for reporting future bugs.

Thank you again for the information and your time, and apologies for any inconvenience caused.

---
